_comment: Clinical decision pathway for Uncomplicated UTI aligned to NHS Pharmacy First eligibility and exclusion criteria.
label: Uncomplicated Urinary Tract Infection (UTI)

questions.text: How long have urinary symptoms been present?
Do you have burning or pain when passing urine?
Do you need to pass urine more often than usual?
Do you have sudden urgency to pass urine?
Do you have blood visible in your urine?
Are you pregnant or could you be pregnant?
Do you have a urinary catheter?
Have you had recurrent UTIs (3 or more in the last 12 months)?
Do you have fever (temperature 38°C or above)?
Do you have pain in your back, side, or flank?

redFlags.description: Fever with flank/back pain suggests upper UTI or pyelonephritis
redFlags.message: Fever with back or side pain can indicate kidney infection. Seek urgent same-day care.

redFlags.description: Vomiting with UTI symptoms indicates possible complicated infection or dehydration risk
redFlags.message: Vomiting with urinary symptoms needs urgent same-day assessment.

eligibilityRules.reason: Pregnancy requires GP or maternity assessment rather than Pharmacy First supply
Breastfeeding requires GP review under local prescribing policy
Catheter-associated urinary infection requires GP management
Visible blood in urine requires GP assessment

eligibilityRules.condition: q2 === false && q3 === false && q4 === false && q5 === false
eligibilityRules.reason: Symptoms do not currently match uncomplicated lower UTI criteria

eligibilityRules.condition: gender === 'Female' && age >= 16 && age <= 64 && q2 === true && (q3 === true || q4 === true || q5 === true) && q7 === false && q8 === false && q9 === false && q10 === false && q6 === false && q11 === false && q12 === false && q13 === false
eligibilityRules.reason: Meets uncomplicated lower UTI criteria for Pharmacy First pathway

outcomeRules.reason: Uncomplicated lower UTI criteria met for pharmacy treatment pathway

outcomeRules.condition: q2 === false && q3 === false && q4 === false && q5 === false
outcomeRules.reason: Symptoms currently do not strongly suggest lower UTI; self-care and monitoring advised
Exclusion or complexity criteria present; GP review required

selfCareAdvice: Drink fluids, use simple pain relief, and monitor symptoms. Seek help if pain worsens, fever develops, or symptoms persist.
pharmacyTreatmentOptions: Nitrofurantoin MR 100mg twice daily for 5 days
safetyNetAdvice: Seek urgent care if fever, flank pain, vomiting, or feeling very unwell develops. Seek GP review if symptoms persist, recur, or worsen.
pathwayPatientDisclaimer: Community pharmacists may assess and treat uncomplicated lower UTI in women aged 16-64 under NHS Pharmacy First where local criteria are met; this tool supports navigation only and does not replace clinical examination or prescribing judgement.
